Hero.title: Software that ships,
Hero.subtitle: I design and build production full-stack apps, AI automation and serverless AWS systems — for businesses that need real results, in English or Spanish.
Hero.chatHint: Or just ask my AI agent 👇

Platforms.title: Built on a stack that scales
Platforms.subtitle: The platforms and technologies I ship with every day — from AWS serverless to fast no-code and low-code.

Agent.title: Talk to my AI agent
Agent.subtitle: It knows my work and can scope your project in seconds.
Agent.placeholder: Ask about pricing, timelines, or what I can build…
Agent.greeting: Hi! I'm Diego's AI agent. Tell me about your project — what are you trying to build?
starters: Can you build a SaaS with payments?
I need to automate emails with AI
How much does a website cost?
Agent.disclaimer: AI agent — responses may be imperfect, and it remembers our chats to pick up where we left off next time. For anything important, I'll connect you with Diego.
Agent.error: Sorry — I couldn't reach the server just now. Please try again in a moment, or use the contact form below.

Services.title: Services
Services.subtitle: Every service is backed by something real I've already shipped to production.

fullstack.title: Custom Full-Stack & SaaS
fullstack.desc: End-to-end web apps and MVPs — real-time platforms, dashboards, internal tools. From idea to deployed product.
fullstack.proof: Backed by ScoreFlow (real-time, WebSocket, role-based access).

cloud.title: AWS-Native Architecture
cloud.desc: Serverless design, cloud migration and cost optimization with Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, S3 and CloudFront.
cloud.proof: 60+ Lambdas and 40 tables running in production today.

ai.title: AI Automation
ai.desc: Personalized email pipelines, content generation, chatbots and lead-nurture flows built directly on Claude and OpenAI.
ai.proof: AI re-engagement system using Claude Haiku + SES on a daily cron.

web.title: High-Performance Websites
web.desc: Conversion-focused landing pages and redesigns tuned for Core Web Vitals in Next.js or WordPress.
web.proof: ~45% checkout conversion and major LCP improvements.

mobile.title: Field, No-Code & Low-Code Apps
mobile.desc: Apps that replace paper for construction, inspections and logistics — custom mobile (React Native) or fast no-code/low-code on AppSheet and Base44, with automated reporting.
mobile.proof: AppSheet field app across 100+ properties with automatic PDF reports.

integrations.title: Payments & CRM Integrations
integrations.desc: Square payments, webhooks, Salesforce Experience Cloud and customer self-service portals connected end to end.

seo.title: Local SEO & Google Business
seo.desc: Local SEO, Google Business Profile and technical SEO/GEO so customers and AI assistants can find you.
seo.proof: A low-cost recurring entry point that compounds over time.

Work.title: Things I've built
Work.subtitle: Real products in production — with real users, payments and uptime.
Work.moreSubtitle: A wider sample across SaaS, AI automation, field ops and low-code.

ai-agent.title: AI Sales Agent
ai-agent.summary: The bilingual AI agent on this very site — it knows my real work through RAG, captures leads, books meetings and remembers returning visitors.
ai-agent.problem: A landing page that only lists services is passive. I wanted a site that actively talks to visitors, answers accurately about my work, and turns interest into captured leads and booked calls — without me in the loop.
ai-agent.architecture: A static Next.js front end calls a Node/Express backend on AWS Lambda. The agent runs on gpt-4o-mini with retrieval-augmented generation (text-embedding-3-small over my real content) for grounding, OpenAI function-calling tools for real actions (lead capture + Amazon SES email, Cal.com meeting booking, live GitHub lookup), and per-visitor long-term memory in DynamoDB. API keys stay server-side and every layer degrades gracefully.
ai-agent.result: A bilingual agent that answers from real data instead of hallucinating, emails leads and meeting confirmations automatically, and recognizes returning visitors — built and shipped to production in phases.

scoreflow.title: ScoreFlow
scoreflow.summary: A real-time judging platform for dance competitions — live scoreboards, multi-round scoring and role-based access for organizers, judges and competitors.
scoreflow.problem: Dance competitions were scored on paper and spreadsheets, causing slow results, errors and no live experience for the audience.
scoreflow.architecture: Next.js 16 SSR on Lambda with a CloudFront origin split (static to S3, dynamic to Lambda). A WebSocket API backed by a DynamoDB connection registry fans out live scores to scoreboards. FastAPI + AWS CDK backend with Cognito auth and fully isolated prod/QA environments.
scoreflow.result: Ran a live event with 21 competitors and 9 judges, with 135 scores cast in real time — zero paper, isolated environments and no data bleed.

dynamic-bachata.title: Dynamic Bachata Platform
dynamic-bachata.summary: A full business platform for a dance studio — online payments, class bookings, automated email and an SEO-tuned site, backed by a 60+ endpoint serverless API.
dynamic-bachata.problem: A growing studio was running on manual signups and disconnected tools — no online payments, no booking system and weak search visibility.
dynamic-bachata.architecture: A Next.js front end on S3 + CloudFront over a 60+ endpoint AWS Lambda API (DynamoDB, Zod-validated), with Square payments, Amazon SES email and isolated prod/QA environments.
dynamic-bachata.result: Scaled from 0 to 200+ paying customers and $51K processed across 864 transactions in 9 months, with major LCP / Core Web Vitals gains.

sky-weekender.title: Bachata Sky Weekender
sky-weekender.summary: A premium, high-converting event site for a 3-day Denver bachata weekender — ticket tiers, Stripe checkout and a cinematic, animated experience.
sky-weekender.problem: A flagship dance event needed a fast, premium site to sell weekend passes online and convey a boutique, high-end feel.
sky-weekender.architecture: A statically-exported Next.js 16 site on S3 + CloudFront with Framer Motion + Lenis, backed by an Express/Lambda API on DynamoDB and Stripe Checkout, deployed to prod and QA via GitHub Actions.
sky-weekender.result: A conversion-focused launch site reaching ~45% checkout conversion, with isolated prod/QA pipelines shipping on every push.

email-campaigns.title: Email Campaign Manager
email-campaigns.summary: A full email-marketing platform — manage contacts, generate campaigns with AI and automate bulk delivery — built for a studio on AWS.
email-campaigns.problem: The studio needed real email marketing — segmented contacts, on-brand copy and bulk sends — without paying for a heavy SaaS suite.
email-campaigns.architecture: A React + Vite app on AWS Amplify behind a Cognito-secured HTTP API, with Node Lambdas drafting copy through OpenRouter AI and sending via Amazon SES — all on DynamoDB with prod/QA isolation.
email-campaigns.result: A self-serve marketing tool that drafts AI campaigns and delivers them in bulk — replacing a paid email SaaS with owned infrastructure.

bachata-crm.title: Dynamic Bachata CRM
bachata-crm.summary: A studio CRM that tracks students, flags who has gone quiet and automatically wins them back with AI-written re-engagement emails.

petary.title: MyPetary Store
petary.summary: A bilingual Amazon-affiliate storefront for men's clothing, perfumes and accessories — a curated catalog with a clean shopping experience, monetized through the Amazon Associates program.

ccc-field-app.title: CCC Field App
ccc-field-app.summary: A no-code AppSheet field app that replaces paper across 100+ properties, capturing structured inspection data and generating PDF reports automatically.

drilled-pier.title: Drilled Pier Field App
drilled-pier.summary: A custom Expo / React Native field-inspection app feeding an AWS Lambda backend that auto-generates structured PDF reports, with branch-mapped CI/CD.

luxury-rides.title: Luxury Rides Denver
luxury-rides.summary: A luxury car-service site built fast on Base44 (low-code) — proof I match the tool to the budget, from full custom builds to rapid low-code delivery.

baychata.title: Baychata
baychata.summary: A full WordPress redesign with a plugin overhaul and performance tuning — cut LCP from 14.9s to 4.6s and lifted mobile PageSpeed from 46 to 72 by removing ~182 KiB of render-blocking CSS.

About.title: Diego Navas Murcia
About.body: I'm a full-stack and cloud engineer based in Denver/Broomfield, Colorado, with three years building software that runs in production. I work natively with AWS — Lambda, DynamoDB, SES, S3, CloudFront, Cognito and API Gateway — and I build automation directly against the Claude and OpenAI APIs. I work fluently in English and Spanish, which means I can serve Denver's businesses and its Hispanic market without losing anything in translation.
About.differentiator: Bilingual EN/ES · Denver-local · ships to production

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: This site is operated by Diego Navas Murcia (HNavas Systems) in Denver, Colorado. Here's what the site collects and how it's handled, in plain language.
Privacy.collectBody: Only what you give us: your name, email and the project details or messages you send through the contact form or the AI chat. The chat also stores a random, anonymous ID in your browser so it can recognize you on a return visit. We don't use tracking cookies, analytics or advertising trackers.
Privacy.useBody: To reply to your inquiry, scope and schedule work, and follow up. The AI chat uses your messages to answer you and to remember your project's context for a return visit.
Privacy.storeBody: On Amazon Web Services, encrypted in transit (HTTPS) and at rest. The chat's memory lives in a DynamoDB database; your message also reaches us by email. API keys stay only on the server, never in your browser.
Privacy.thirdBody: We rely on a few trusted providers that may process your information: OpenAI (generates the chat replies), Amazon Web Services (hosting, database, email), and Google (the inbox where we receive your message). If you book through our scheduling link, that's handled by Cal.com under their own policy. We never sell or rent your data.
Privacy.retainBody: The chat's memory profile auto-expires after about 90 days of inactivity. Emails you send are kept until you ask us to delete them.
Privacy.choicesBody: Email us anytime to see or delete the data we hold about you. You can also clear the chat's anonymous ID by clearing your browser storage.
Privacy.contactBody: Questions about this notice or your data? Email us and we'll help.
